narrativeContinuity: One work packet travels through models, runtimes, controls, connectors, and governance.

recurringSymbols.meaning: a user task, prompt, or data unit moving through the system
the visible context available to the model or agent
policy, permission, guardrail, or governance boundary
standardized connection surface such as MCP

compositionSystem: Left visual metaphor plus right explanatory panel plus bottom series rail.

videos.title: What Is an LLM?
videos.objective: A large language model predicts tokens from context; it is not a database lookup.

videos.title: LLM Billing
videos.objective: Cost follows model work, even when a product wraps usage as seats, credits, or subscriptions.

videos.title: Probabilities and Evaluation
videos.objective: LLM output is sampled from probabilities; evaluation decides whether the sample is good enough.

videos.title: What Is an Agent?
videos.objective: An agent is a goal-directed loop that observes, acts, reads results, and continues until it should stop.

videos.title: What Is a Guardrail?
videos.objective: A guardrail enforces policy around inputs, outputs, or actions.

videos.title: What Is a Harness?
videos.objective: A harness is the runtime shell that turns a raw model into a usable assistant or agent.

videos.title: What Is a Harness Hook?
videos.objective: A hook is an event-driven interception point inside the harness lifecycle.

videos.title: What Is a Harness Plugin?
videos.objective: A plugin packages reusable harness behavior so teams can install and govern it.

videos.title: What Is a Skill?
videos.objective: A skill is on-demand domain expertise: instructions, resources, and scripts for a repeatable task.

videos.title: What Is MCP?
videos.objective: Model Context Protocol is standardized plumbing between AI clients and external tools or data.

videos.title: AI Alternatives
videos.objective: Choose assistants by workflow gravity: where context lives, what actions matter, and how control works.

videos.title: Observability
videos.objective: Observability makes agent behavior inspectable through traces, spans, events, costs, and outcomes.

videos.title: Instruction Layers
videos.objective: Instruction layers are the rule stack that shapes behavior before the model sees the task.

videos.title: Tools and Permissions
videos.objective: Tool permission is the boundary between helpful suggestion and real-world action.

videos.title: Data Governance
videos.objective: Data governance decides what the assistant may see, retain, train on, and reveal.
